commentaryText.text:
I joined PW (PhysicsWallah) as a Frontend Developer Engineering Intern for 180 days as a 2nd year student - I don’t know if there could have been any experience better than this one to start off my tech career!

While I sit to pen it down - I am not able to decide what to talk about first!

My connection with PW (PhysicsWallah) is not a new one, as just 2 years back I was preparing for #JEE!

I have seen the brand grow having a very large user base covering around 98% of India's pin codes - I had a rough idea of how large and efficient the tech team working at the backend would be.

And I was right !
I worked closely with the User Acquisition team which in itself included great people - both by professionalism and nature - highly skilled, driven and very helpful. 

Being among them taught me how corporate tech truly works while sharpening my dev skills.

Every day felt like a game – starting with Daily Scrum Calls led by Ravindra Pandey Sir, picking tasks from Jira, coordinating with members from Design Team and Product Team, or Senior Developers, and getting approvals or unblocking help from Samyak Jain Sir in huddles, to finally sending the Merge Requests for the day!

Each sprint came with new learnings – from building small widgets to handling complete landing page components, solving Linting Issues for Staging env codebase, fixing P-0 UI & QA bugs, understanding the internal CMS to even building small features for it and many more tasks.

Being an intern let me closely interact with everyone in the team - each one of them having multiple unique things to observe, learn and incorporate.

These 6 months flew by – but they were the most productive and enriching ones yet. Working with real developers on real products gave me knowledge no course could.

At last I want to thank Gopal Sharma Sir, Anurag Tiwari Sir and Janishar Ali Sir for the opportunity, all the team members Ravindra Pandey Sir, Keshav Ashta Sir, Shilpa Chourasia Ma'am, Samyak Jain Sir, Sanjit Kurar Sir, Aniket Rana Sir, Manjeet Singh Sir, Uttam Sharma Sir, Lakshita Sethi  Ma'am, suneet sachan, Manan Khandelwal, Aman Raj Gupta and all others Dinker Chaudhary Sir, Amrit Raj Sir, Ankur Gupta Sir whom I got a chance to interact with and learn from in this period.

Special mention for Alakh Pandey (PhysicsWallah) Sir and Prateek Maheshwari Sir, for taking one YT channel to a Unicorn Organization that has helped many of us!

PS : I finally got my hands on MAC 😃 (provided to members of Tech Engg Teams which I was manifesting 10 months back) and learnt Version Control truly for the first time!

commentaryText.text:
Change is the only constant.

Left home for the first time to 
Bangalore on Oct 6, 2023

Doubt in one pocket, Belief in the other.

And in the Journey, saw...

Growth & Dips.  
Clarity & Fog.  
Momentum. Drift.  
Conviction. Hesitation.

854 days later, Leaving Again for 
Bangalore on Feb 6, 2026  

Not to restart.  
To meet the next iteration of myself

The path reveals itself only to the foot that steps.

Nobody claps until you 𝗪𝗜𝗡!!
In your 20's you don't get called ambitious for taking the unconventional path.
You get called irresponsible.

Until it works. Then suddenly nobody was ever against you.

Here's what the timeline actually looks like:
18 — "you're just 𝗻𝗼𝘁 𝗳𝗼𝗰𝘂𝘀𝗲𝗱 enough"
19 — "why can't you 𝗯𝗲 𝗺𝗼𝗿𝗲 𝗹𝗶𝗸𝗲 𝘆𝗼𝘂𝗿 𝗰𝗹𝗮𝘀𝘀𝗺𝗮𝘁𝗲𝘀"
20 — "this is so 𝗿𝗶𝘀𝗸𝘆, think about your future"
21 — "okay but what's your backup plan"
22 — "I just don't want 𝘆𝗼𝘂 𝘁𝗼 𝗿𝗲𝗴𝗿𝗲𝘁 it"
23 — "wow, I always knew 𝘆𝗼𝘂 𝗵𝗮𝗱 𝗶𝘁 in you"

Same person. Same choices. Same path.
The only thing that changed was the proof.

I know this because I'm in it right now.
Skipped classes. Started freelancing when everyone was chasing internships. ₹3k per month in first sem to 𝟭𝟳𝘅 𝗻𝗼𝘄 — no plan, just stayed on the path when it made no sense to.

From the outside I know exactly what that looks like.

The sad part isn't the doubt.
The sad part is most people quit because of the silence.

They had the Notion pages, the saved reels, the 2am voice notes.
They just didn't have the willingness to look irresponsible for long enough.

That's the only gap.
Not talent. Not timing. Not resources.

Just the tolerance to be misunderstood before you're understood.
The timeline comes for everyone.

𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗾𝘂𝗲𝘀𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗶𝘀 𝘄𝗵𝗲𝘁𝗵𝗲𝗿 𝘆𝗼𝘂'𝗿𝗲 𝘀𝘁𝗶𝗹𝗹 𝗼𝗻 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗽𝗮𝘁𝗵 𝘄𝗵𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘁 𝗱𝗼𝗲𝘀.

I'm on a journey to put a FULLSTOP.
And I call you to be a part of it, to put fullstop on all the hurdles in your growth!

#youngentrepreneurs #freelancejourney #buildingInpublic #unconventionalpath #creatoreconomy #entrepreneurmindset #selfmade #startuplife #growthmindset #youthentrepreneur #freelancer #solopreneur
